intentionalDifference: The renderer expands shorthands and lowercases keyword values during parsing; the bridge preserves the raw shorthand and value spelling.
Both parsers recover the semicolon inside the data URL, but the renderer stores the URL payload without the url(...) wrapper while the bridge preserves it.

css: @font-face { font-family: 'Phase Zero'; src: url(phase-zero.woff2); } @keyframes pulse { from { opacity: 0; } to { opacity: 1; } } .animated { opacity: .5; }
intentionalDifference: The renderer parser stores font-face and keyframe metadata; the bridge style-rule cache strips those at-rules and exposes them through a separate CSSOM parser.
